title: What is the Medicare levy?
description: The Medicare levy is an amount you pay in addition to the tax you pay on your taxable income.
keywords: Medicare levy, Medicare, levy, what is the medicare levy, how much is the medicare levy

content_blocks.text: The Medicare levy helps fund some of the costs of Australia's public health system known as Medicare. The Medicare levy is 2% of your taxable income .
Media: Medicare levy and Medicare levy surcharge explained https://tv.ato.gov.au/ato-tv/media?v=bi9or7od7oq6a3 External Link ( Duration: 00:56)

You may get a reduction or exemption from paying the Medicare levy, depending on you and your spouse's income and circumstances. You need to consider your eligibility for a reduction or an exemption separately.
We calculate your Medicare levy when you lodge your tax return. Generally, the pay as you go amount your employer withholds from your salary or wages includes an amount to cover the Medicare levy.
You can use the Medicare levy calculator to work out your Medicare levy.
The Medicare levy surcharge (MLS) is a separate levy to the Medicare levy. Depending on your personal circumstances, you may need to pay the MLS in addition to the Medicare levy.
Example: working out how much Medicare levy to pay
Matthew earns an income of $76,000 annually. He has $1,000 of allowable deductions.
Matthew is single and doesn't have any dependants. Matthew isn't entitled to the Seniors and pensioners tax offset (SAPTO) and isn't eligible for any Medicare levy exemptions or reductions.
Matthew will pay a Medicare levy of 2% of his taxable income.
Matthew works out his taxable income as:
Assessable income − allowable deductions = taxable income
The amount of Medicare levy Matthew pays is:
The Medicare levy is automatically calculated when Matthew completes his tax return.

content_text:
The Medicare levy helps fund some of the costs of Australia's public health system known as Medicare. The Medicare levy is 2% of your taxable income .
Media: Medicare levy and Medicare levy surcharge explained https://tv.ato.gov.au/ato-tv/media?v=bi9or7od7oq6a3 External Link ( Duration: 00:56)
You may get a reduction or exemption from paying the Medicare levy, depending on you and your spouse's income and circumstances. You need to consider your eligibility for a reduction or an exemption separately.
We calculate your Medicare levy when you lodge your tax return. Generally, the pay as you go amount your employer withholds from your salary or wages includes an amount to cover the Medicare levy.
You can use the Medicare levy calculator to work out your Medicare levy.
The Medicare levy surcharge (MLS) is a separate levy to the Medicare levy. Depending on your personal circumstances, you may need to pay the MLS in addition to the Medicare levy.
## Example: working out how much Medicare levy to pay
Matthew earns an income of $76,000 annually. He has $1,000 of allowable deductions.
Matthew is single and doesn't have any dependants. Matthew isn't entitled to the Seniors and pensioners tax offset (SAPTO) and isn't eligible for any Medicare levy exemptions or reductions.
Matthew will pay a Medicare levy of 2% of his taxable income.
Matthew works out his taxable income as:
Assessable income − allowable deductions = taxable income
$76,000 − $1,000 = $75,000
The amount of Medicare levy Matthew pays is:
$75,000 × 0.02 = $1,500
The Medicare levy is automatically calculated when Matthew completes his tax return.
source_title: What is the Medicare levy? | Australian Taxation Office
source_description: The Medicare levy is an amount you pay in addition to the tax you pay on your taxable income.
